summary_en: Stock exchange: abc to acquire Bond Co. as wholly-owned subsidiary via simplified stock exchange (no shareholder approval required for abc). Exchange ratio 1:35,710. abc to issue 714,200 new ordinary shares. Effective June 12, 2026. Strategic rationale: entry into amusement poker business; community-based entertainment expansion.
title_en: Notice Regarding Acquisition of Complete Control of Bond Co., Ltd. through Simplified Stock Exchange
translation_en_brief:
On May 21, 2026, abc Co., Ltd. announced its acquisition of Bond Co., Ltd. as a wholly-owned subsidiary via simplified stock exchange. The exchange ratio is fixed at 1:35,710; abc will issue 714,200 new ordinary shares to Bond shareholders. Abc's board approved the stock exchange contract same-day; Bond shareholders approved the transaction at a special meeting on May 21, 2026. The exchange is effective June 12, 2026 (tentative). As a simplified stock exchange under Company Law Article 796(2), abc did not require shareholder approval.

The strategic rationale is to enter the amusement poker business—a growing community-based entertainment market in Japan—and expand abc's entertainment portfolio. Bond operates poker events, tournaments, and player communities. Abc plans to integrate Bond's shop operations and event expertise, launch large-scale poker tournaments through its subsidiary Camelot, and develop digital settlement and proprietary tokens (digital membership certificates). Revenue is projected to combine shop operations, major tournament fees, and token economics. No cash outlay is required under the stock-for-stock structure, which also aligns Bond and abc shareholders' interests for long-term value creation.
